page_content:
UMD Alerts alert.umd.edu

Identification Cards

Photo ID cards should be carried at all times and are used by all students to withdraw books from the libraries, to be admitted to most campus events, and as a general form of identification on campus.

The UMD Alerts System is a mass, urgent notification system, comprised of a variety of methods by which the University can notify students, faculty and staff of an active, major campus emergency. UMD students, faculty and staff are automatically registered through their campus-assigned email address. Add a cell number and other email address by logging in and editing the settings at alert.umd.edu.

Every faculty/staff/student is issued one photo ID card for the duration of their time at the university. Knowledge of your UID number is required to obtain a UMD ID card. Students may obtain a UMD ID card upon registration; a valid photo ID (e.g. State Driver’s License or Passport) must be presented at the Office of the University Registrar Front Counter Services. Students who are not currently registered may not receive a UMD ID card.
